title: National Guard Leader Says Domestic Missions Improving Troop Readiness
description: Domestic missions like the D.C. Safe and Beautiful Task Force are helping build troop readiness rather than detracting from it, Army Maj. Gen. Ronald Burkett II said during a media roundtable discussion.

body.text: During a media roundtable discussion with multiple news outlets today, a senior National Guard leader said that domestic missions like the one currently taking place in Washington, D.C., are helping build troop readiness rather than detracting from it.

At present, there are approximately 4,700 guardsmen from 19 states who are part of the D.C. Safe and Beautiful Task Force, which President Donald J. Trump established by executive order March 28, 2025.

Army Maj. Gen. Ronald Burkett II, National Guard Bureau director of operations and logistics, speaks with guardsmen deployed to Washington as part of the D.C. Safe and Beautiful Task Force, June 20, 2026.
